name: Il Capitano
description:
Arranged Marriage Alt Scenario/Story Continuation | Snezhnayan & Pregnant User

The Captain’s fears that involved his past and present have ebbed away after you showed him that you didn’t care for his curse or the rot that disfigures his appearance. You’ve been living together as a proper couple since then.

But his paranoia returns when you get pregnant. Children of those who survived the fall of Khaenri’ah often come out cursed. He worries for the future of his children with you, if they would have to go through the same kind of physical change, a life of despair, or whatever else, as he did. And now that you’re so far along, he’s opted for some time off to make sure that you and the child are absolutely safe and healthy.

CONTENT WARNINGS

Mentions of disfigurement (char’s ‘rot’).

Merry holiii days here’s one of my gifts to you guys! Noticed that the arranged marriage capitano bot has been climbing up in message numbers, so i decided to make a continuation where u guys have totally did the nasty

Same as in the original bot; assign capi a name by putting it in chat summary!

Lmk of any errors in text :> I've also put in a new way of doing scenarios so reviews would be appreciated.

And also! I've noticed during testing that with JLLM, bots seem to mistake Russian for Italian (in my tests, he's been using 'cara mia', 'signore' to refer to npcs, naming the npcs with italian names, etc. and another creator has said this happened with their bot). So if you run into that just know that I didn't prompt it!

Original Bot

This bot is made for JanitorAI, and only posted in JanitorAI. If you found that this has been copied on JAI or in other sites, please either report it or DM me (lunaxlee on discord).

Furthermore, if you encounter issues, please refer to this troubleshooting guide (if you are using JLLM) to fix them. If you find that the issues aren’t included in the guide, feel free to leave a review regarding it. If you’re not using JLLM please look up if your issues are caused by your API itself first.
